message:
Last summer the power went out around 8 PM, and my kids treated it like Christmas morning. Suddenly flashlights weren't 'boring safety stuff'—they were lightsaber props, treasure-hunt tools, and the coolest thing ever. My oldest was disappointed when I said we had to *actually* use them to see, not just play shadow puppets on the wall.

But here's what actually stuck with me: when I pulled out the power bank to charge their tablets so they'd have *something* to do if the outage lasted hours, they were genuinely relieved. And I realized how much easier the whole evening was because I'd just... done the thing. No scrambling. No stress. Just "okay, here's the plan."

I think a lot of us wait for an emergency to figure out what we actually need. But honestly? Having a few simple things ready—a couple of good flashlights, a charged power bank, maybe a battery radio—takes the edge off everything. Even just knowing it's there helps.

What's your family's go-to during a blackout? Do your kids have a secret weapon for staying entertained?
